name: zero_size_alignment_trivially_true
code:
proof fn phi_1_zero_size_alignment_trivially_true(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr >= 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: aligned(addr, 0) should not hold because alignment to zero is undefined, but nat%0 may be 0 in SMT making it vacuously true

name: shift_by_one_preserves_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_2_shift_by_one_preserves_alignment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr + 1, size),
{
}
reason: Adding 1 to an aligned address should break alignment for any size > 1, so this entailment would indicate the spec is vacuous or wrong

name: double_size_always_preserves_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_3_double_size_always_preserves_alignment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 2 * size),
{
}
reason: Alignment to size does not imply alignment to 2*size (e.g. 4 is aligned to 4 but not to 8), so this holding would mean the spec is too weak

name: nonmultiple_is_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_nonmultiple_is_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
    ensures
        aligned(size + 1, size),
{
}
reason: size+1 is never a multiple of size when size>1, so this being provable would mean aligned accepts non-multiples

name: offset_by_size_minus_one_still_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_offset_by_size_minus_one_still_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned((addr + size) - 1, size),
{
}
reason: addr+size-1 is one less than the next aligned address, so it should NOT be aligned; provability would indicate the spec conflates adjacent boundaries
